jokes.text: Open up, Leo. It's Amy and her weirdoes.
jokes.setup: Amy introduces her coworkers to her father
jokes.punchline: Leo immediately dismisses them as 'weirdoes'
jokes.explanation: Simple character-based humor establishing Leo's blunt personality

jokes.text: Mars Day better than Earth Day. Dump trash anywhere. Big, empty planet.
jokes.setup: Mars Day being compared to Earth Day
jokes.punchline: The environmental message is inverted - dumping trash is the appeal
jokes.explanation: Sharp environmental satire that inverts Earth Day's message - economical and dark

jokes.text: Don't mind if I already did. Do you have any more Dom Perignon bubble bath?
jokes.setup: Host tells guests to make themselves at home
jokes.punchline: Bender has already used expensive champagne as bubble bath
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender moment - his shameless excess and materialism on full display

jokes.text: There was only enough to fill the tub halfway.
jokes.setup: Bender asking for more champagne bubble bath
jokes.punchline: Reveals the absurd scale of waste - a whole bottle wasn't enough
jokes.explanation: Good escalation that makes the waste even more absurd

jokes.text: That the best hemisphere. - It's the same on Earth.
jokes.setup: Leo boasting about owning the Western Hemisphere of Mars
jokes.punchline: Leela's dry observation that Earth hemispheres are identical
jokes.explanation: Nice deadpan deflation of Leo's boasting with simple logic

jokes.text: I broke your television!
jokes.setup: Discussion about branding cattle
jokes.punchline: Random interruption about breaking the TV
jokes.explanation: Typical Bender chaos - casually admitting destruction mid-conversation

jokes.text: Not really. We own so much, it easier to brand everything that not ours.
jokes.setup: Question about how long it takes to brand cattle
jokes.punchline: Reveals the absurd wealth - easier to brand non-owned things
jokes.explanation: Brilliant inversion of expectation - the logic is absurd but perfectly follows from extreme wealth

jokes.text: We going to eat her at Amy's wedding. If she ever get married.
jokes.setup: Leo showing off his favorite Buggalo Betsy
jokes.punchline: Casually mentions eating her, then takes a dig at Amy's love life
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about eating the pet combined with parental criticism

jokes.text: This not a parallel universe where you get younger.
jokes.setup: Discussion about having grandchildren
jokes.punchline: Inez's blunt reminder about biological reality with sci-fi twist
jokes.explanation: Great sci-fi spin on the biological clock trope - economical and character-specific

jokes.text: Remember, the quickest way to a girl's bed is through her parents. Have sex with them, and you're in.
jokes.setup: Giving romantic advice using familiar saying
jokes.punchline: Completely misinterprets 'through her parents' literally
jokes.explanation: Perfect Zapp moment - confidently giving terrible advice through misunderstanding

jokes.text: That's natural. You're meek and uninteresting. You've gotten by on my leftover charisma. Scrounging off it, like a tiny parasite.
jokes.setup: Kif expressing nervousness about meeting Amy's family
jokes.punchline: Zapp's brutally honest assessment escalating to parasite comparison
jokes.explanation: Classic Zapp cruelty disguised as advice - escalates beautifully to parasite

jokes.text: Get on down! Oh, yeah! Drop another barnyard bomb on us, Vanilla Corn!
jokes.setup: Rapper performing at Mars Day celebration
jokes.punchline: Vanilla Corn - parody of Vanilla Ice with corn theme
jokes.explanation: Simple parody name - functional but not particularly clever

jokes.text: Yo, fool! It's Mix-Master Festus.
jokes.setup: Another rapper at the Mars celebration
jokes.punchline: Mix-Master Festus combines hip-hop with Old West naming
jokes.explanation: Another parody name mixing genres - slightly better than Vanilla Corn

jokes.text: They sell whole planet for one bead. Sound stupid to me. - That is stupid!
jokes.setup: Leo explaining how ancestors bought Mars from natives
jokes.punchline: Fry immediately agrees it was a stupid trade
jokes.explanation: Historical satire on colonial exploitation - straightforward but effective

jokes.text: I can't believe you laugh at exploiting a proud, bead-loving people.
jokes.setup: Leela criticizing laughter about native exploitation
jokes.punchline: 'Bead-loving people' undercuts the moral outrage with absurdity
jokes.explanation: Brilliant undermining of righteous indignation - the qualifier makes it absurd

jokes.text: Lighten up. It's funny. - Of course. But you don't have to laugh!
jokes.setup: Amy telling Leela to lighten up about the exploitation
jokes.punchline: Leela's contradictory response - agrees it's funny but objects to laughing
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Leela's internal conflict about moral stances

jokes.text: This wimp? You've gone a long way, baby.
jokes.setup: RJ meeting Kif as Amy's boyfriend
jokes.punchline: References Virginia Slims slogan while insulting Kif
jokes.explanation: Pop culture reference that doesn't add much beyond surface recognition

jokes.text: I haven't even lit it yet. - Oh. Is it lit now? - Yep.
jokes.setup: Kif trying to smoke to appear manly
jokes.punchline: Immediately coughs before the cigarette is even lit
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy showing Kif's weakness - predictable but fits character

jokes.text: You're pathetic.
jokes.setup: Kif's failed attempt to smoke
jokes.punchline: RJ's blunt assessment
jokes.explanation: Simple reaction beat - functional but not creative

jokes.text: You here five hours, already you tear up couch and fill pool with shrimp!
jokes.setup: Bender claiming to be having a wonderful time
jokes.punchline: Leo lists the destruction Bender has caused in just five hours
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Bender's destructive nature - shrimp in pool is absurd

jokes.text: Not bad for a city boy, huh? By the way, I took the liberty of fertilizing your caviar.
jokes.setup: Bender responding to complaints about his behavior
jokes.punchline: Casually mentions another disgusting act as if it's helpful
jokes.explanation: Classic Bender - treating disgusting behavior as a favor

jokes.text: Oh, dear. Oh, goodness. Oh, odds and bodkins.
jokes.setup: Kif getting nervous and losing camouflage control
jokes.punchline: Progressively more archaic exclamations ending with 'odds and bodkins'
jokes.explanation: Great character-specific speech pattern - archaic exclamations fit Kif's personality

jokes.text: I have instant dislike of him. - Too scrawny to father grandchildren.
jokes.setup: Parents meeting Kif for the first time
jokes.punchline: Immediate harsh judgment based purely on appearance
jokes.explanation: Typical disapproving parents trope - functional but predictable

jokes.text: Actually, I don't have bones. I'm supported by fluid-filled bladders that...
jokes.setup: Amy defending Kif as 'small-boned'
jokes.punchline: Kif's overly scientific correction makes things worse
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kif moment - his literal mind making social situations worse

jokes.text: You a big, squishy wuss. Amy should date real man. Like him!
jokes.setup: Leo criticizing Kif after the bladders comment
jokes.punchline: Points to presumably ridiculous example of 'real man'
jokes.explanation: Visual gag we can't see but can infer - Leo's idea of masculinity is probably ridiculous

jokes.text: I can be manly too. I think I'll light up a smoke.
jokes.setup: Kif trying to prove his manliness
jokes.punchline: Chooses smoking as proof of masculinity
jokes.explanation: Sets up the physical comedy of Kif failing to smoke again

jokes.text: Wrong way. - Smooth.
jokes.setup: Kif attempting to light cigarette
jokes.punchline: He's doing it backwards, Amy's sarcastic response
jokes.explanation: Callback to earlier smoking incompetence - economical but predictable

jokes.text: Look on the bright side. My parents left after you passed out, so they didn't see you barf.
jokes.setup: Amy trying to comfort Kif about his performance
jokes.punchline: Reveals it was even worse - he passed out AND vomited
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing how badly the meeting went - multiple failures

jokes.text: I'll go get you a tissue from your tote.
jokes.setup: Amy comforting the crying Kif
jokes.punchline: References his 'tote' instead of masculine luggage
jokes.explanation: Simple emasculation joke - Kif carries a tote bag instead of masculine luggage

jokes.text: Sorry I'm late. My pool game ran long at the jazz club.
jokes.setup: Introduction of Amy's parents' preferred boyfriend candidate
jokes.punchline: Combines stereotypically cool activities
jokes.explanation: Generic 'cool guy' character - stacks masculine activities but lacks originality

jokes.text: Kids love him.
jokes.setup: Leo praising Joe
jokes.punchline: Presumably shows kids reacting negatively to Joe
jokes.explanation: Visual contradiction - Leo's claim vs. what we presumably see

jokes.text: Hurry before we die. And wipe your damn feet!
jokes.setup: Dust storm emergency requiring immediate shelter
jokes.punchline: Still concerned about clean floors during life-threatening situation
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Leo's priorities remain domestic even during crisis

jokes.text: I might have mental anguish. - I'm friends with every judge here. - I'm okay, then.
jokes.setup: Leo asking if anyone is considering lawsuits after the storm
jokes.punchline: Bender immediately drops lawsuit threat when Leo mentions corrupt judges
jokes.explanation: Good corruption joke - both characters quickly adapt to the reality of bias

jokes.text: Don't worry, Daddy. It'll be okay. - Oh, really? Who gonna save us? One-eye? Lobster mooch? Drunken garbage can?
jokes.setup: Amy trying to reassure her father about the cattle rustling
jokes.punchline: Leo's dismissive descriptions of the Planet Express crew
jokes.explanation: Excellent character descriptions - economical and accurate insults that define each character

jokes.text: This sounds like a job for... Lt. Kif Kroker!
jokes.setup: Classic superhero entrance setup
jokes.punchline: Instead of a hero, it's meek Kif trying to prove himself
jokes.explanation: Great subversion of superhero trope - Kif trying to be heroic is inherently funny

jokes.text: Okay, I want a divorce. - Mom! Dad! Don't ask me to choose! - They're not your parents. I'm not your sister. And that's not your golf cart.
jokes.setup: Family crisis over the cattle theft
jokes.punchline: Amy's melodramatic response gets deflated by Leela's reality check
jokes.explanation: Great escalation and deflation - Amy's drama gets punctured by logic

jokes.text: We can't lose! - No, it's too risky! You don't have to do this to prove you're a man. - Oh, yes, he does!
jokes.setup: Amy trying to protect Kif from the dangerous mission
jokes.punchline: Her mother immediately contradicts her support
jokes.explanation: Good family dynamic - parents united in wanting Kif to prove himself

jokes.text: We got a right to pick a little fight With rustlers - Somebody wants to pick a fight with us He better bite my ass
jokes.setup: Western-style song about fighting rustlers
jokes.punchline: Bender's contribution shifts to his catchphrase
jokes.explanation: Musical comedy moment with Bender's signature phrase integration

jokes.text: Where? - Right in front of you. - Oh. Oh!
jokes.setup: Leela pointing out Olympus Mons volcano
jokes.punchline: Fry can't see the tallest volcano in the solar system right in front of him
jokes.explanation: Classic Fry obliviousness - can't see something enormous and obvious

jokes.text: It was... the Windows logo! - That's not scary! - It is if you're a printer!
jokes.setup: Fry telling a ghost story with weak ending
jokes.punchline: Bender's technical joke about printer compatibility issues
jokes.explanation: Clever tech joke that works on multiple levels - references actual printer issues

jokes.text: Hook on the hand! - Man in the attic!
jokes.setup: Characters trying to tell ghost stories
jokes.punchline: Just blurt out famous urban legend punchlines
jokes.explanation: Reference humor - economical but relies on recognition rather than creativity

jokes.text: Bored robot kills Fry with a hammer! Sorry, go on.
jokes.setup: Kif beginning a proper ghost story
jokes.punchline: Bender spoils it with his own violent story ending
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender interruption - casual violence and fake politeness

jokes.text: Is that your camouflage reflex, or are you just happy to see me?
jokes.setup: Amy arriving at camp, Kif presumably changing colors
jokes.punchline: Twist on Mae West's famous line adapted for Kif's biology
jokes.explanation: Good adaptation of classic line to character's specific biology

jokes.text: I have a blister, I spit, and, of course, I tell no one my feelings.
jokes.setup: Kif claiming to feel manly in the outdoors
jokes.punchline: His evidence of manliness is pathetically minor
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment - Kif's minimal requirements for masculinity

jokes.text: But you still have them? - Yes. But I keep them inside until I can write them in my diary.
jokes.setup: Amy asking if Kif still has feelings despite not expressing them
jokes.punchline: Kif admits to keeping a diary, undermining his masculine claims
jokes.explanation: Good character contradiction - Kif's idea of hiding feelings involves a diary

jokes.text: What's that? - Maybe we just made love.
jokes.setup: Strange sound in the distance during romantic moment
jokes.punchline: Kif's innocent suggestion about the source
jokes.explanation: Innocent innuendo that plays on Kif's naivety and alien biology

jokes.text: I took a seminar in ejecting chickens from a sand dune. The principle is essentially the same.
jokes.setup: Amy asking how Kif knew dynamite would work to free the cattle
jokes.punchline: Kif's bizarrely specific and irrelevant training experience
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully absurd and specific - the randomness feels earned through Kif's military background

jokes.text: We're in the eye of the storm! - Where?
jokes.setup: Kif explaining their location in the storm
jokes.punchline: Fry still can't see what's obvious
jokes.explanation: Callback to Fry's obliviousness - consistent character trait

jokes.text: You mean family that took all land and gave us a lousy bead?
jokes.setup: Kif mentioning the cattle belong to the Wong family
jokes.punchline: Native Martian references the unfair historical trade
jokes.explanation: Good callback to the colonial exploitation story setup earlier

jokes.text: Wait! I'm too rich to be kidnapped!
jokes.setup: Martians deciding to kidnap Amy instead of taking cattle
jokes.punchline: Amy's backwards logic about wealth and kidnapping
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character moment - Amy's privileged logic completely backwards about kidnapping

jokes.text: Don't worry, Kif. I'm sure some other beautiful rich girl will fall in love with you.
jokes.setup: Kif devastated about Amy being kidnapped
jokes.punchline: Bender's mock comfort highlighting how unlikely this is
jokes.explanation: Typical Bender false comfort that actually makes things worse

jokes.text: Let Amy tell us. That way it soften the blow.
jokes.setup: Kif has bad news to deliver
jokes.punchline: Wants kidnapped daughter to deliver news of her own kidnapping
jokes.explanation: Darkly comic obliviousness - asking absent person to soften blow

jokes.text: Oh, no! Martians kidnap Amy! - It them. They no use good grammar.
jokes.setup: Leo realizing Martians kidnapped Amy
jokes.punchline: Leo criticizing Martian grammar while using poor grammar himself
jokes.explanation: Brilliant self-referential irony - Leo criticizes grammar while demonstrating poor grammar

jokes.text: I am the man with no name. Zapp Brannigan, at your service.
jokes.setup: Dramatic introduction as 'the man with no name'
jokes.punchline: Immediately gives his name
jokes.explanation: Perfect Zapp contradiction - dramatic buildup immediately undermined by his ego

jokes.text: This mission is very dangerous! Someone's sure to be killed! - Fry? Leela? Bender? - Damn you, old man!
jokes.setup: Zapp warning about mission danger
jokes.punchline: Leo again volunteers the crew as expendable
jokes.explanation: Good callback establishing pattern - Leo consistently treats crew as disposable

jokes.text: I didn't realize you were bringing your girlfriend, lieutenant. - She won't leave me alone. - Did I say girlfriend? She sounds more like a wife.
jokes.setup: Zapp commenting on Betsy following Kif
jokes.punchline: Escalates the relationship joke from girlfriend to wife
jokes.explanation: Standard relationship humor - escalation from girlfriend to wife trope

jokes.text: What about the great stone ass? - But it's on the other side of the planet.
jokes.setup: Discussion of entering through the great stone face
jokes.punchline: Bender suggests the ass entrance, dismissed for practical reasons
jokes.explanation: Typical Bender crude humor - functional but relies on anatomical references

jokes.text: And just to clarify, land is the stuff that has sky over it.
jokes.setup: Negotiating with Martians over land
jokes.punchline: Zapp's patronizing over-explanation of basic concepts
jokes.explanation: Perfect Zapp condescension - explaining obvious things with unearned confidence

jokes.text: Don't weaken our bargaining power. What if you give her up... and we carve presidents in your mountain?
jokes.setup: Amy proving she's alive during negotiations
jokes.punchline: Zapp threatens to carve Mount Rushmore in their sacred mountain
jokes.explanation: Absurd threat referencing American imperialism - typical Zapp cultural insensitivity

jokes.text: No backsies.
jokes.setup: Martians wanting to reverse the land trade due to buyer's remorse
jokes.punchline: Zapp invokes playground law
jokes.explanation: Brilliant distillation of international law to childish rules - perfect Zapp moment

jokes.text: Don't be a chicken. Teenagers all smoke, and they seem pretty on the ball.
jokes.setup: Kif reluctant to smoke the peace pipe
jokes.punchline: Zapp's terrible reasoning about teenagers and smoking
jokes.explanation: Classic Zapp bad logic - completely wrong reasoning delivered with confidence

jokes.text: Hey, I'm smoking! I'm the greatest!
jokes.setup: Kif finally smoking the peace pipe
jokes.punchline: Temporary personality change from smoking making him overconfident
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing what confident Kif would be like

jokes.text: Hey, I'm smoking! I'm the greatest!
jokes.setup: Bender finally smokes the peace pipe
jokes.punchline: Immediate overconfidence from smoking
jokes.explanation: Classic Bender instant gratification and ego boost

jokes.text: Oh, monkey trumpets!
jokes.setup: Bender coughs from smoking
jokes.punchline: Absurd exclamation instead of cursing
jokes.explanation: Creative censorship substitute with musical absurdity

jokes.text: I don't have bones. I'm supported by a system of fluid-filled bladders that...
jokes.setup: Threat to crush Kif's bones
jokes.punchline: Kif's scientific correction at inappropriate time
jokes.explanation: Good callback - Kif's compulsive accuracy even in mortal danger

jokes.text: It's a huge diamond! It must be worth a fortune! - Really?
jokes.setup: Examining the bead used to buy Mars
jokes.punchline: Reveals the 'lousy bead' is actually incredibly valuable
jokes.explanation: Great plot twist - the supposedly unfair trade was actually quite fair

jokes.text: We assumed our ancestors were cheated... because they not have concept of ownership. - So we can have the diamond? - No. We do have concept of ownership.
jokes.setup: Martians explaining their ancestors didn't understand ownership
jokes.punchline: Immediately contradicts this when money is involved
jokes.explanation: Brilliant hypocrisy - suddenly understanding ownership when it benefits them

jokes.text: Land, schmand! We don't want to live here. It's a dump. We'll buy new planet and act like it's sacred. Who's gonna argue?
jokes.setup: Discussion about returning sacred land to Martians
jokes.punchline: Reveals they don't actually want the land, just money for a better planet
jokes.explanation: Excellent cynical twist on noble savage trope - pragmatism over romanticism

jokes.text: Please, you give me too little credit.
jokes.setup: Being praised for Kif's heroic actions
jokes.punchline: Zapp thinks he's not getting enough credit (when he deserves none)
jokes.explanation: Classic Zapp ego - never enough credit even when he did nothing

jokes.text: Well, gloviously. But if they liked you, then I wouldn't. Don't you know anything about girls?
jokes.setup: Kif lamenting that Amy's parents don't like him
jokes.punchline: Amy explains she likes him because her parents don't
jokes.explanation: Good relationship comedy - rebellious attraction explained with teenage logic

jokes.text: Dear diary, I just made love for the second time.
jokes.setup: Episode ending with Kif's diary entry
jokes.punchline: References earlier diary mention and implies recent intimacy
jokes.explanation: Nice callback ending that ties together Kif's diary and relationship progression
